objective: Expand open-only LAB-bench LitQA2 synthesis testing from 10 to 20 questions before spending more on judge/synth process variants.
rationale: The 10-question slice is useful for debugging but too small for reliable model differentiation or synthesis claims.
The open-only panel oracle was 7/10, but majority and Kimi-judge/GLM-synth both scored 6/10.
The key unresolved failure is preserving lone useful minority signals, especially Gemma on Q6.
Correlation and rescue analytics should be computed before additional LLM adjudication spend.

first_adjudication_process_after_analyti.name: disagreement_only_minority_defense
first_adjudication_process_after_analyti.note: Do not rerun judge/synth over unanimous rows. Debate/minority-defense should explicitly test lone dissenters rather than merely warning not to follow majority.
